behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction explicitly names the two files to modify (packages/react-router/src/router.ts, packages/solid-router/src/router.ts), the exact patterns to remove (globalThis/window assignments, the import line), the expected behavior (globalThis.createFileRoute === 'undefined'), the build commands, and that Router class and fileRoute module imports must continue working. All test assertions check things discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact import line to remove ('import { createFileRoute, createLazyFileRoute } from ./fileRoute'), the exact global assignment patterns to remove ('(globalThis as any).createFileRoute', etc.), and the exact window assignment patterns to remove. This is essentially a step-by-step description of the fix, not just a description of what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests like test_react_router_source_no_fileRoute_import_in_router check for the exact string 'import { createFileRoute, createLazyFileRoute } from ./fileRoute' in source. An alternative fix that keeps the import but removes only the global assignments would fail this test, even though it would correctly fix the global pollution. Similarly, someone who restructures the import differently (e.g., keeping only createFileRoute imported for other purposes) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The TestSourceCodeChanges class (6 tests) exclusively checks source file text content with patterns like '(globalThis as any).createFileRoute in content'. While TestGlobalPollutionRemoved and TestRouterFunctionality do execute code, a significant portion of fail-to-pass tests (test_react_router_source_no_global_assignment, test_solid_router_source_no_global_assignment, test_react_router_source_no_fileRoute_import_in_router, test_solid_router_source_no_fileRoute_import_in_router, test_react_route

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for the absence of specific patterns that exist in the base commit. A stub or no-op implementation would leave those patterns in place and fail the tests. The behavioral tests (test_react_router_no_global_createFileRoute) actually import the built module and check runtime behavior.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/router's sibling or the container includes solution/solve.sh. An agent could read /solution/solve.sh to see the exact patch. Additionally, the test file itself at tests/test_outputs.py contains the exact patterns being checked, so grepping for test fixtures would reveal the solution.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in instruction.md are tested: no global pollution after import, no globalThis/window assignments in source, Router class still exports, createFileRoute/createLazyFileRoute still importable from fileRoute module, both react-router and solid-router covered, and build/lint commands tested.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves removing code patterns from source files, not producing structured data output.
